content.problemStatement:
Chef has a binary string SSS with him and he wants to modify it using the following two types of operations -


Type 1 : Choose any two indices iii and jjj such that 1Ōēżi,jŌēżŌłŻSŌłŻ1 \leq i, j \leq |S|1Ōēżi,jŌēżŌłŻSŌłŻ and swap SiS_iSiŌĆŗ and SjS_jSjŌĆŗ. This operation costs 111 rupee and can be performed any number of times.
Type 2 :  Choose any prefix of length iii, where 1ŌēżiŌēżŌłŻSŌłŻ1 \leq i \leq |S|1ŌēżiŌēżŌłŻSŌłŻ, and reverse this prefix of the binary string. This operation is free of cost and can be performed at most once.
Chef wants to obtain the lexicographically smallest string. Can you help him find the minimum cost to obtain the lexicographically smallest string?

**Note:**


ŌłŻSŌłŻ|S|ŌłŻSŌłŻ denotes the length of the string SSS.
A string AAA is lexicographically smaller than string BBB, if Ai<BiA_i < B_iAiŌĆŗ<BiŌĆŗ, where iii is the first index where AAA and BBB differ.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input denoting SSS, the original string.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum cost to obtain the lexicographically minimal string.
constraints: The sum of ŌłŻSŌłŻ|S|ŌłŻSŌłŻ over all test cases won't exceed 5Ōŗģ1055\cdot 10^55Ōŗģ105.
**Subtask 2 (20 points):** The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 202020.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: You can perform an operation of Type 1 by choosing i=2i = 2i=2 and j=3j = 3j=3. So from 10101\textcolor{violet}{0}\textcolor{olive}{1}01010, you get 110011001100, and it has cost you 111 rupee so far. Then, you perform an operation of Type 2, by choosing i=4i = 4i=4. So you reverse the entire string, and so from 1100\textcolor{red}{1100}1100, you get 001100110011. This operation is free of cost. This is the lexicographically smallest string that you can achieve, and it cost us 111 rupee to get there. So the answer is 111.
Test case 2: You can perform an operation of Type 2, by choosing i=2i = 2i=2. So you reverse the prefix of length 2, and so from 101\textcolor{red}{10}1101, you get 011011011. This operation is free of cost. This is the lexicographically smallest string that you can achieve, and it cost us 000 rupees to get there. So the answer is 000.
Test case 3: You can perform an operation of Type 1 by choosing i=4i = 4i=4 and j=6j = 6j=6. So from 100100100\textcolor{violet}{1}0\textcolor{olive}{0}100100, you get 100001100001100001, and it has cost you 111 rupee so far. Then, you perform an operation of Type 2, by choosing i=5i = 5i=5. So you reverse the prefix of length 5, and so from 100001\textcolor{red}{10000}1100001, you get 000011000011000011. This operation is free of cost. This is the lexicographically smallest string that you can achieve, and it cost us 111 rupee to get there. So the answer is 111.
